figure.title: TX Path Security Architecture
figure.purpose: Shows the cryptographic key and counter management for the transmit (TX) path feeding into the AES-GCM Crypto Engine.

technical_summary: A Key Derivation Function (KDF) uses Active & alternate master keys (2x32B per destination) to generate derived keys for different stream types.
The streams include Request, Read Response, and Write Response.
For each destination accelerator (up to 1023), the engine receives a 32B Derived Key, a 4B IV counter, and an 8B epoch counter for each stream type.
These inputs feed into the AES-GCM Crypto Engine to secure the TX data.

components.name: AES-GCM Crypto Engine
components.description: Hardware encryption block for the TX path.

critical_facts: The TX security architecture maintains separate cryptographic contexts (keys, IVs, epoch counters) for up to 1023 destination accelerators.
